page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It has a 128 MB L3 cache, 8 MB L2 cache, and 512 KB L1D cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% via stress-ng. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is measured at 129.76 GB/sec, while write bandwidth reaches 65.71 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance shows strong text generation speeds on medium-sized LLM models and top-tier performance on small-model text generation, alongside average multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density for compute-heavy workloads without the premium of GPU-accelerated hardware. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide reliable, non-burstable performance. This profile is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, image processing, medium-scale LLM inference, web serving, and general-purpose compute tasks requiring consistent memory bandwidth and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 128 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier Passmark memory scores and strong single-core CPU capabilities, alongside efficient text generation for small and medium language models. Multi-core execution, database operations, and static web serving perform at average levels. Qualitatively, the instance offers balanced resource density and reliable, non-burstable compute power. It is suited for software compilation, image processing, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory performance for web serving and language model inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance delivering balanced memory bandwidth and strong single-core performance for web serving and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 128 GB RAM.
Top-tier memory performance and strong single-core CPU capabilities.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Highly efficient text generation for small and medium LLM models.
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and web serving.
